heading: Number of passengers
label: 3501 Number of passengers
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource section (U.S. Code)350146:404–146:45146:45246:462Section 3501 requires that a vessel carrying passengers, including a passenger vessel as well as a small passenger vessel (except a ferry), shall have on the certificate of inspection a statement as to the number of passengers the vessel is permitted to carry. It also provides penalties for carrying passengers in excess of the number permitted.

Amendments1985—Subsec. (a). , struck out the comma after “(except a ferry)”.Pub. L. 99–36, § 1(a)(2)(A)Subsec. (c). , substituted “carries more passengers than the number of passengers permitted by the certificate of inspection” for “violates subsection (b) of this section”.Pub. L. 99–36, § 1(a)(2)(B)
Effective DateChapter effective , see , set out as a note under .Apr. 15, 1984section 2(g)(1) of Pub. L. 98–89section 3101 of this title
amendments: Amendments1985—Subsec. (a). , struck out the comma after “(except a ferry)”.Pub. L. 99–36, § 1(a)(2)(A)Subsec. (c). , substituted “carries more passengers than the number of passengers permitted by the certificate of inspection” for “violates subsection (b) of this section”.Pub. L. 99–36, § 1(a)(2)(B)

children.label: a
children.text: Each certificate of inspection issued to a vessel carrying passengers (except a ferry) shall include a statement on the number of passengers that the vessel is permitted to carry.
$100 for each passenger in excess of the number of passengers permitted.

children.label: c
children.text: An owner, charterer, managing operator, agent, master, or individual in charge of a vessel that knowingly carries more passengers than the number of passengers permitted by the certificate of inspection also shall be fined not more than $100, imprisoned for not more than 30 days, or both.

children.label: d
children.text: The vessel also is liable in rem for a penalty under this section.

children.label: e
children.text: An offshore supply vessel may not carry passengers except in an emergency.
